steps: Warm sesame oil self-massage (abhyanga) before showering
Gentle warm-water rinse, avoid cold plunges
Grounding yoga or gentle stretching, 10-15 minutes
Warm, moist breakfast (e.g. spiced oatmeal) by 8:00 AM
Regular meal times to steady vata's variability
Warm, nourishing dinner by 7:00 PM
Wind-down with warm milk or herbal tea and consistent sleep by 10:00 PM
Cool or lukewarm shower, avoid very hot water
Moderate-intensity yoga or a walk in cooler morning air, 15-20 minutes
Substantial breakfast by 7:30 AM to manage strong pitta digestion
Largest meal at midday when digestive fire peaks
Cooling foods and adequate hydration through the afternoon
Light dinner by 7:00 PM
Wind down away from screens/heat, sleep by 10:30 PM

kapha.wake_time: 5:30 AM (before kapha's natural sluggishness sets in)
steps: Brisk dry-brushing or vigorous self-massage to stimulate circulation
Warm or cool energizing shower
Vigorous exercise or sun salutations, 20-30 minutes
Light breakfast, or skip if not hungry, favoring warm spiced tea
Largest meal at midday, light and warm dinner by 6:30-7:00 PM
Stay active through the afternoon to counter sluggishness
Sleep by 10:00 PM, avoid daytime napping

ritucharya.winter: Favor warm, unctuous, grounding foods; regular oil massage; moderate but sustained exercise.
ritucharya.summer: Favor cooling, hydrating foods; avoid peak-heat exertion; lighter oils.
ritucharya.monsoon: Favor warm, freshly cooked, easily digestible food; support digestion (agni) which weakens in humidity.
ritucharya.spring: Favor light, warming, pungent foods to counter kapha accumulation from winter; increase activity.
ritucharya.autumn: Favor grounding, moist, warm foods as vata increases with dryness and wind.
